name: The Ark's Chicken Marsala
recipeInstructions:
Note: If you don't want to go to the expense of demi-glace, you can use twice as much Burgundy. Dissolve ½ teaspoon cornstarch in wine before adding it to the pan and increase the butter to 1 ½ tablespoons.
Use a meat cleaver to tenderize and flatten the chicken breast. Dredge in the flour. Heat margarine over medium-high heat in a medium saucepan. When the margarine is melted, place the chicken in the pan and cook until it is golden brown on all sides, about 3 minutes. Add garlic to the pan and turn the chicken over. Continue cooking for another minute. Then add mushrooms and wine. Cook until the wine has reduced by half. Remove the chicken from the pan once it is cooked through and place it on a serving plate. Add Demi-glace and butter to the saucepan and stir to melt the butter. Pour the sauce over the chicken and serve.
Nutritional information per serving: 552 calories; 55g protein, 11g carbohydrates, 29g fat (48%), 1.3g fiber, 175mg cholesterol, 337mg sodium
Source: The Ark, Davie, Florida; Miami Herald; 12/14/95
